passage.title: Recycling Corner Redesign
passage.text: The eco club wanted younger students to sort recycling more correctly. At first, the team used one poster that listed every rule in small writing, but students still dropped paper and plastic into the wrong bins. A practice class said "I can see the bins, but I am not sure which one is right." After hearing that, the team gave each bin one strong color and one simple symbol and placed the same color signs above the matching shelves. They also set a reminder card by the door to the recycling corner so students saw a real example before making a choice. On the next test day, more students sorted their materials correctly on the next clean-up day. The team noticed that clear examples helped more than long rule lists. Because of that result, the eco club planned to redesign more green spaces with the same system.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Writing long reports about class rules
Improving eco signs so younger students can sort materials correctly
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Students still put materials into the wrong bins
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place next to each bin?
choices: Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
One example item
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does hesitation mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
A pause caused by not feeling sure
A room that is easy to clean
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It removed every sign from the area
The new system showed clearer differences between each eco choice
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer system in more eco areas
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
